title: Process Engineer
description: We are working with a leading life sciences company in South Dublin to recruit a Process Engineer. The Process Engineer will support syringe filling and single\-use technology (SUT) development for drug product formulation and filling operations. This role will play a key part in major expansion initiatives, including the qualification and characterization of filling technologies and implementation of new single\-use systems for new syringe filling lines. The successful candidate will provide technical leadership and process development expertise across commercial large and small molecule drug product manufacturing. **Key Responsibilities** * Lead the development, qualification, and implementation of single\-use systems for drug product formulation and filling processes in collaboration with site and global teams. * Support filling process characterization activities, including recipe development, protocol authoring, execution, data analysis, and technical reporting. * Provide technical expertise for sterile processing, filling operations, process characterization, and commercial drug product manufacturing support, including technology transfer activities. * Lead or support investigations, deviation management, root cause analyses, risk assessments, and CAPA implementation to ensure robust process performance and compliance. * Troubleshoot drug product processing technologies and equipment using data\-driven approaches to identify and resolve manufacturing challenges. * Partner with Operations, Engineering, Quality, and external vendors to support new technology introductions, process validation activities, and regulatory compliance requirements. **Key Requirements** * Bachelor's degree in Science, Engineering. * 10 years experience in support of Commercial Protein Drug Product (DP) processing in specific areas such as Sterile Processing, Process Characterization, Tech Transfer (to commercial DP sites) or Validation. * Strong knowledge of aseptic manufacturing, drug product processing, cGMP requirements, and global regulatory expectations. * Technical expertise in formulation, mixing, filtration, filling processes, and single\-use system design and implementation. * Experience with drug product filling technologies, including peristaltic pumping, time\-pressure filling systems, and filling process characterization. * Demonstrated leadership, problem\-solving, technical writing, and communication skills, with the ability to effectively lead cross\-functional teams and complex projects. Due to the high volume of applications, we may not be able to respond to every applicant. AscendPM do not forward candidate CVs to clients without prior consent.

title: QC Microbiology Analyst
description: I am recruiting for a QC Microbiology Analyst to join a leading biopharmaceutical company located in North Dublin. The analyst will be responsible for supporting Drug Substance and Drug Product site activities related to Bioburden and Endotoxin in\-process testing and sample processing. **Key Duties and Responsibilities** * Carrying out In\-Process testing of critical lot related samples to support Manufacturing activities. * Performing Bioburden testing on Utility samples, Drug Substance and Raw Materials within required hold times. * Performing Bacterial Endotoxin Testing on Utility samples, Drug Substance and Drug Product within required hold times * Performing Growth Promotion testing of media lots entering the laboratory. * Carrying out identifications of Microorganisms isolated post testing activities. * Carrying out the Minute Mouse Virus by PCR for in\-process samples. * Performing compendial verification, method transfer/validation, and routine testing of in\-process, drug substance and drug product samples in accordance with applicable procedures and cGMP requirements. * Completing all laboratory documentation in a timely and accurate manner. * Compliance with Standard Operating Procedures and Registered Specifications. * Assisting in authoring and reviewing documentation. * Providing support to other departments to ensure qualification and production schedules are adhered to. * Reviewing paperwork, and reconciliation of analysis performed in the laboratory. * Ensuring that any alert/action limit excursions are investigated according to site procedures. * Adherence to and improvement upon lean lab and 6S practices. * Ensuring a high standard of housekeeping and safety is maintained in the laboratory. * Performing any other activities as indicated by the Microbiology Manager. **Qualifications and Experience** * The ideal candidate should hold a minimum of a Level 8 degree in Microbiology or related discipline. * At least 1 years’ experience in a pharmaceutical / healthcare laboratory or related technical function. * A strong background in Microbiology and aseptic technique is desirable but not essential. * Sterility experience is desirable but not essential. * Experience in Bioburden and Endotoxin testing is desirable but not essential. * Experience using MODA, CIMS, LIMS, LES and Veeva Vault is desirable but not essential. * The successful candidate must demonstrate excellent written and verbal communication skills. * The ability to work in a team based collaborative environment is essential.

title: Quantity Surveyor
description: **Shape a future with purpose at Arup in Cork or Dublin.** Arup’s purpose, shared values and collaborative approach has set us apart for over 80 years, guiding how we shape a better world. **Driving Commercial Excellence** Our Procurement and Cost Management team is redefining how value is delivered across infrastructure and building projects. By combining deep technical expertise with a collaborative mindset, we’re helping clients make smarter investment decisions and embed sustainability, innovation, and behavioral best practice into every stage of the project lifecycle. **The Opportunity** Join Arup’s growing Project \& Commercial Management (PCM) team in Cork, where you’ll play a pivotal role in shaping the future of cost and commercial advisory across Ireland. As a Quantity Surveyor, you’ll help lead a dynamic, multidisciplinary team focused on delivering high\-impact infrastructure and building projects that prioritise sustainability, value, and innovation. This is a chance to influence how we deliver commercial excellence, from mentoring junior colleagues and refining procurement strategies, to driving whole\-life value and embedding behavioural best practice into day\-to\-day operations. You’ll be part of a team that’s not only expanding in size, but also in ambition with opportunities to contribute to strategic growth, thought leadership, and the evolution of Arup’s commercial capability. **As a Quantity Surveyor At Arup, You Will** * Support the preparation of cost estimates, cost plans, and tender documentation across infrastructure and building projects to help inform project budgets and procurement decisions. * Assist with procurement and contract administration activities, including preparing tender documents, reviewing contractor submissions, measuring works, and supporting valuations. * Contribute to cost control and commercial reporting by helping prepare cost updates, forecasts, and financial summaries that support effective project management. * Work closely with multidisciplinary teams, attending meetings, site visits, and workshops to build understanding of project delivery, stakeholder needs, and commercial priorities at different project stages. * Develop practical experience in construction contracts (e.g., NEC, Public Works Contracts, FIDIC) while supporting senior colleagues in delivering high\-quality commercial outputs and maintaining accurate project records. **Does this role inspire you?** **We’re Looking For Someone Who** * Has relevant qualifications in Quantity Surveying, Construction Economics, or a related discipline, with early career experience welcomed. * Has a basic understanding of cost management principles, procurement processes, and contract administration. * Is keen to develop technical and commercial skills through hands\-on project experience. * Demonstrates strong numerical, analytical, and organisational skills, with attention to detail. * Has good communication skills and the ability to work effectively within a team environment. * Has familiarity with MS Office applications and an interest in developing skills in estimating tools (e.g. CostX, CostOS) **Why Arup?** At Arup, we believe in rewarding our members for their contributions to our shared success. As a trust\-owned company, we have the freedom to pursue meaningful work that aligns with our purpose and values. We also offer a unique profit share program, allowing our members to share in the results of our collective efforts. **Benefits That Work For You** * Professional Growth \& Development: Benefit from continuous learning opportunities, training programs, and mentorship to enhance your skills and advance your career at Arup. * Global Opportunities: Collaborate with colleagues worldwide and explore potential short\-term or long\-term assignments at other Arup offices and project locations. * Financial Well\-being \& Ownership: Receive a competitive salary and benefits package and share in Arup's success through our global profit\-sharing programme. * Well\-being and Culture: at Arup, your well\-being matters. We foster a culture of care, inclusion, and collaboration, supported by global initiatives and local programmes that promote mental health, social connection, and a sense of belonging. At Arup, you belong to an extraordinary collective where individuality is encouraged. Our strength comes from how we respect, share, and connect our diverse experiences, perspectives, and ideas. You'll have the opportunity to do socially useful work that has meaning – to Arup, to your career, and to the clients and communities we serve. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer and are committed to building a workplace that reflects the rich diversity of the world around us. We actively promote a culture of inclusion where everyone feels welcome, respected, and valued. We welcome applications from talented individuals of all backgrounds, regardless of age (within legal limits), gender identity or expression, disability, ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, or any other protected characteristic. We are committed to making our recruitment process and workplaces accessible. If you need any assistance or adjustments, please contact recruitment.europe@arup.com . We're here to support you. **Do you want to join us in shaping a better world?** https://www.arup.com/careers/

title: Real Estate Solicitor | Permanent | Dublin
description: After a sustained period of significant growth, the real estate team at Philip Lee LLP is looking to hire a 1\-3PQE solicitor. **The Firm** Philip Lee LLP is one of Ireland’s leading commercial law firms with offices in Dublin, Cork, London and New York. The firm has built a team of top tier lawyers who can advise on every aspect of business, both in Ireland and internationally. The firm acts for companies across all industry sectors and the lawyers at the firm are recognised for their ability to work through complex challenges and provide innovative solutions. Philip Lee LLP is the sole Irish member of Multilaw, a consortium network of over 10,000 lawyers in 100 countries worldwide. **The Role** **Key Responsibilities:** * Assisting with advising property teams on the acquisition and funding of land for major residential and commercial developments. * Assisting with advising developers on the legal delivery of turnkey and forward\-funded developments. * Assisting with advising developers and landowners in judicial review proceedings challenging planning decisions. * Assisting with advising on challenges to the inclusion of lands on Residential Zoned Land Tax (RZLT) maps. * Assisting with the structuring of multi\-unit residential and mixed\-use developments, including management company and service charge regimes. * Assisting with complex High Court and Commercial Court litigation arising from development, land use, and property disputes. * Assisting with the negotiation of complex settlements in multi\-party property and planning disputes to achieve commercially workable outcomes. * Assisting with advising borrowers and developers on restructuring, forbearance, and distressed property scenarios. **About You** The successful candidate will: * be a 1\-3 PQE solicitor with experience in real estate; * be naturally collaborative and understand the importance of being part of a team; * prioritise attention to detail; * display commercial awareness and understand what is important to clients; and * have strong communication skills. **Why Apply?** This is an excellent opportunity to join a growing real estate practice. The firm offers a collaborative and inclusive culture with a genuine focus on professional development and progression. **Benefits** At Philip Lee LLP we are committed to maintaining an inclusive, supportive environment in which all of our people feel valued and can enjoy doing what they love, working together to provide a first\-class service to our clients. Specific initiatives to support our employees include but are not limited to: * associate and partner development programmes; * the opportunity to join various firm committees, including our social, wellness, green, pro bono and diversity committees; * subsidised yoga and fitness classes; and * numerous social events throughout the year. Please submit your CV in confidence to hoshaughnessy@philiplee.ie.

title: Recruitment Consultant
description: We are looking for an ambitious Recruitment Consultant/Senior Consultant to join one of our busiest \& most successful teams based in our fabulous Dublin office. This is a great opportunity to join a high performing team who offer indepth market knowledge \& long standing client \& candidate relationships from which you would be able to leverage further success. **Your new role** The responsibilities include: Building long term connections with your client base \- introducing yourself \& Hays to new prospective client over calls, emails \& F2F meetings. Seeking out new businesses to work with, attend networking events \- raise your profile !Developing and maintaining strong relationships with key decision makers. Sourcing, screening and recruiting top\-tier candidates in a candidate short market \- become their 'go to' contact \& provide a consultancy service to them Drafting reports, ensuring compliance across all activities, and participating in internal projects. Contribute to the development and growth of the business **What you'll get in return :** As a multi award\-winning employer of choice, we believe as part of the employee and employer deal at Hays, we can give you exciting career development opportunities to support your aspirations. Our culture is a reason why so many of our people choose to stay and grow their careers with Hays. We are big on collaboration and driven to connect with each other on a genuine human level. We call it the Hays spirit. You will experience this inclusive, motivating energy in every part of our business. In addition, you will enjoy: Uncapped individual commission paid upon invoice every 4 weeks 23 days holiday plus take your birthday for free Regular performance\-based incentive programmes including all\-expense paid international trips A culture underpinned with trust, which is based on expertise, training, collaboration, inclusivity and doing the right thing Industry\-leading learning and development to maximise your performance and advance your career every step of the way A global brand with established PSL agreements, RPO/MSP accounts and warm client relationships An adult approach to working with hybrid working models and flexible working hours The opportunity to take your career across the globe with our global mobility offering Diverse range of employee support networks, wellbeing and volunteering initiatives as well as wider benefits We know talented people want to work with the best: people, brand, tools, technology and infrastructure. They also want career development. Our culture is shaped and created by these features. We're also committed to doing the right thing, together. As a progressive employer, we believe in doing the right thing for our people our customers and the communities in which we live and work. We're constantly working to deliver on our commitments to social purpose, equity, diversity \& inclusion and sustainability.

title: Reinstatement Agent - GNI
description: **Are you looking to become part of a collaborative team whilst maximising your potential to grow and develop within your career? Apply today.** **The Role.** GMC are currently looking for an experienced **Reinstatement Agent** to join our team. The Reinstatement Site Agent is responsible for managing and coordinating reinstatement activities associated with Gas Networks Ireland (GNI) infrastructure works, including excavation reinstatement, permanent road and footpath restoration, and ensuring full compliance with GNI specifications and local authority requirements. **Key Duties and Responsibilities will include but are not limited to:** **Key Responsibilities:** Oversee day\-to\-day reinstatement works across multiple GNI project sites, ensuring they are completed safely, on time, and to the required standards. Coordinate reinstatement crews, subcontractors, traffic management teams, and materials suppliers. Conduct site inspections to verify compliance with GNI specifications, permits, method statements, and local authority conditions. Enforce HSEQ policies in line with GNI requirements. Coordinate with local authorities regarding permits, road opening licences (ROL), traffic management plans, and inspections. Support planning of reinstatement operations, including resource allocation, material ordering, and sequencing of works. Demonstrate strong commercial awareness by managing costs effectively, identifying opportunities for efficiency, and ensuring value for money in all reinstatement activities. **The Candidate: Required Experience and Competencies:** Proven experience in reinstatement, utilities, civil engineering on gas infrastructure projects. Strong understanding of road reinstatement, compaction processes, materials standards, and traffic management. Knowledge of GNI specifications, road opening licensing (MapRoad), and utility reinstatement standards. Excellent organisational and site coordination skills. Strong communication and stakeholder\-management abilities. Full, clean driving licence. **Become a part of something bigger.** At the heart of GMC is our people, from engineers through to customer service and we aim to work collaboratively towards our common goals. GMC is an equal opportunity employer, and we aim to recruit and retain like\-minded people from diverse backgrounds with a wide range of expertise. If you require any specific accommodations, we will make every reasonable effort to support you throughout the recruitment and selection process. Please contact us at careers@gmcirl.com. In GMC we foster a work culture that promotes collaboration and learning, building an environment where our people have the capacity to maximise their potential whilst gaining the opportunity to continuously develop. **Our Future.** Since the founding of GMC in 1981 by Gerry McCloskey, GMC has grown to be Ireland's leading Civils and Utilities company and the future for GMC is bright. We have engaged with our staff and other stakeholders to agree and publish our core values. One of our core values is to *"Focus on the Future"* . Our growth to date reflects the confidence that our customers have in our service delivery, in the innovative approach that we bring to how we do our work, and to our culture of safety and quality. We have ambitious plans to develop our footprint in Ireland and overseas but understand that every decision we make contributes to people's quality of life throughout generations, and we don't take that responsibility lightly as we build Ireland's infrastructure of tomorrow, today.

title: Release Engineer
description: **About Us:** Tech Mahindra offers technology consulting and digital solutions to global enterprises across industries, enabling transformative scale at unparalleled speed. With 150,000\+ professionals across 90\+ countries helping 1100\+ clients, TechM provides a full spectrum of services including consulting, information technology, enterprise applications, business process services, engineering services, network services, customer experience \& design services, AI \& analytics, and cloud \& infrastructure services. It is the first Indian company in the world to have been awarded the Sustainable Markets Initiative’s Terra Carta Seal, in recognition of actively leading the charge to create a climate and nature\-positive future. **Role: Release Engineer** **Experience: 5 to 9 years** **Work from office: 3 days per week** **Job location: Dublin, Ireland** **Release Management** * Own end\-to\-end release orchestration across all environments (Dev cloud → Stage → ITF → Perf → MTF → Prod) * Define and enforce release calendars, gates, and quality criteria * Coordinate cross\-team dependencies, go/no\-go decisions, and production readiness * Drive release standardisation and automation, reducing manual steps over time **Lower Environment Ownership** * Own availability, stability, and health of all non\-prod environments * Define SLAs for uptime, data freshness, and usability * Lead root cause analysis for recurring instability patterns **Environment Deployments** * Own and standardise deployment processes into lower environments * Ensure deployments are repeatable, automated, and observable * Eliminate environment drift and configuration inconsistencies **Branching Strategy** * Define and enforce branching model and release flows * Ensure alignment between branching, CI/CD pipelines, and release cadence * Govern adoption across all InControl service teams **Developer Testing \& Tooling** * Enable consistent local development and testing workflows * Provide tooling and patterns for local service execution, integration testing, and debugging * Reduce dependency on shared environments for early\-stage testing **Automation \& Test Execution** * Own nightly regression execution framework and stability * Drive triage and root cause ownership model for failures (infra vs code vs data) * Improve signal\-to\-noise ratio and test reliability **CI/CD \& Pipeline Engineering** * Own design, implementation, and evolution of CI/CD pipelines across InControl * Define standards, templates (“golden paths”), and best practices * Drive improvements in build time, deployment frequency, and reliability **Pipeline \& Tooling Platform Ownership** **Own lifecycle and strategy for:** * Jenkins * Forge * Chef Habitat * MKS (POC → decision → rollout) * Rationalise tooling footprint and drive platform convergence * Ensure tooling is scalable, secure, and well\-integrated **Engineering Standards \& Tech Mandates** * Own rollout and enforcement of cross\-cutting technical mandates: * Spring Boot upgrades * UTC standardisation * Other platform\-wide changes * Provide tooling, automation, and tracking to ensure adoption at scale **How To Apply:** It's easy to apply online; you just need a copy of your up\-to\-date CV and to follow the step\-by step process. Don't worry if you need to make changes \- you'll have the opportunity to review and edit your work on the final page, or you can also share resume directly to provided email address. We look forward to receiving your application! Tech Mahindra is an Equal Employment Opportunity employer. We promote and support a diverse workforce at all levels of the company. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, religion, color, sex, age, national origin or disability. All applicants will be evaluated solely on the basis of their ability, competence, and performance of the essential functions of their positions.

title: Remote Software Engineer
description: **About Us:** Based in San Francisco, California, Turing is the world’s leading research accelerator for frontier AI labs and a trusted partner for global enterprises deploying advanced AI systems. **Turing supports customers in two ways:** first, by accelerating frontier research with high\-quality data, advanced training pipelines, plus top AI researchers who specialize in software engineering, logical reasoning, STEM, multilinguality, multimodality, and agents; and second, by applying that expertise to help enterprises transform AI from proof of concept into proprietary intelligence with systems that perform reliably, deliver measurable impact, and drive lasting results on the P\&L. **Project Overview:** As a Software Engineering evaluator, you will create cutting\-edge datasets for training, benchmarking, and advancing large language models, collaborating closely with researchers. This includes curating code examples, providing precise solutions, and making corrections in Python, JavaScript (including ReactJS), C/C\+\+, Java, Rust, and Go; evaluating and refining AI\-generated code for efficiency, scalability, and reliability; and working with cross\-functional teams to enhance enterprise\-level AI\-driven coding solutions. **What Does a Typical Day Look Like?** * Working on AI model training initiatives by curating code examples, building solutions, and correcting code in Python, JavaScript (including ReactJS), C/C\+\+, Java, Rust, and Go. * Evaluate and refine AI\-generated code to ensure that it is efficient, scalable, and reliable. * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams to enhance AI\-driven coding solutions against industry performance benchmarks. * Build agents that can verify the quality of the code and identify error patterns. * Hypothesize on steps in the software engineering cycle (prototyping, architecture design, API design, production implementation, launch, experiments, monitoring, operational maintenance) and evaluate model capabilities on them * Design verification mechanisms that can automatically verify a solution to a software engineering task. **Required Skills:** * 3\+ years of software engineering experience. * Strong expertise in building full\-stack applications and deploying scalable, production\-grade software using modern languages and tools. * Deep understanding of software architecture, design, development, debugging, and code quality/review assessment. * Excellent oral and written communication skills for clear, structured evaluation rationales. **Engagement Details:** * **Commitment** : flexible engagement, minimum 10 hrs/week, up to 40 hrs/week (partial PST overlap required) * **Type** : Contractor (no medical/paid leave) * **Duration** : 1 month (starting next week; potential extensions based on performance and fit) * **Location:** Candidates must be based out of US, Canada or WEU countries (UK, Netherlands, Italy, Germany, …) **Note: As part of assessments you will go through an AI video interview.** **After applying, you will receive an email with a login link. Please use that link to access the portal and complete your profile.** Know amazing talent? Refer them at turing.com/referrals, and earn money from your network.

title: Reporting Analyst [Temp 12 months]
description: **Vacancy Number:** 28574 **Category:** Trading **Job Type:** Full\-time Position Description Reporting to the Reporting Team Lead, the Reporting Analyst plays a key role within a commercially focused Pricing and Trading team. The role involves delivering accurate, timely tariff books and reporting to multiple stakeholders, while supporting wider Pricing and Trading activities. The team manages Electric Ireland’s reporting across market segments, including tariff book distribution for price changes (commercial or regulatory), customer volume reporting to Generation \& Trading, and provision of financial data for month\-end, forecasts, and budgeting. It also supports price reviews, monitoring processes, and regulatory reporting. This role suits an analytical, motivated individual seeking experience in a commercial environment. It requires strong collaboration with Pricing, Finance, Regulation, Business \& Residential Markets. This is a 12\-month fixed\-term maternity leave cover role. Key Responsibilities Deliver accurate tariff books to support pricing and billing Provide customer volume data for Electric Ireland and ESB Generation \& Trading Produce timely month\-end, quarter\-end, and year\-end financial reports Complete all regulatory reporting requirements Conduct billing analysis and market monitoring to inform trading decisions Assist with Green Source Verification, Fuel Mix Disclosure, and audits. Support annual emissions reporting (ETS2\) Contribute to Renewable Heat Obligation (RHO) reporting Experience and Qualifications Essential 2 \+ years’ experience of the energy industry, and knowledge of wholesale or retail electricity markets Third level degree in a related discipline, or equivalent work experience. Proven record of working as part of a team, providing support and guidance to other team members Strong analytical and numerical skills Ability to take initiative where appropriate, with ability to work to tight deadlines whilst maintaining accuracy and attention to detail. Strong Microsoft Office skills specifically in the use of complex Excel spreadsheets Proven ability to collaborate and engage with multiple stakeholders Excellent communication and presentation skills (both written and verbal). Desirable Experience of working in pricing and / or trading environment. Knowledge of TPS, DFS systems. Competent in the use of computer\-based modelling packages with experience in VBA, SPSS and SQL Server. Location This position will be based in Swift Square, Santry. Smart Working at ESB is designed to make ‘in\-person’ time together as purposeful as possible while also enabling the effective use of digital tools and hybrid arrangements to preserve our commitment to flexibility. The successful candidate may work flexibly within the jurisdiction where they have been employed, with attendance at the workplace for in\-person collaboration at least two days per week . All Smart Working arrangements are subject to regular review based on the operational requirements of the role, the team, and the business. Reporting To Reporting Team Lead, Pricing and Trading Why Work with Us? Opportunity to lead Ireland’s transition to net zero carbon future Career development through mentoring and training Corporate Social Responsibility Opportunities Sports and Social Clubs Networking opportunities Credit Union Generous Pension Access to staff well\-being programmes Generous parental leave entitlements Strong values\-based and inclusive culture Strong commitment to diversity, equity and inclusion **Great team environment working to our Core Values:** Courageous, Caring, Driven and Trusted Salary €49,800 \- €58,600 per annum (depending on experience) Closing Date 30th June 2026 Interview Candidates who are shortlisted for interview will complete a Behavioural Interview based on each of the following Behaviours, which underpin how we work at ESB and are critical to delivering our strategy: Accountable and Ethical Collaborative Growing and Adaptive Proactive Respectful and Supportive More information available on Our People Promise Notes Your application will be held in reserve for 6 months should you be suitable for the role. The successful candidate will be required to evidence their right to work in the Republic of Ireland prior to commencement of employment. Diversity, Equity and Inclusion Statement ESB is committed to being an equal opportunities employer. We welcome applications from all sections of society and ensure that no one is discriminated against on the grounds of race, religion or belief, ethnicity or nationality, disability, age, citizenship, marital status, domestic or civil partnership status, sexual orientation or gender identity, or any other basis as protected by law. Applicants who anticipate requiring assistance or reasonable accommodations for any part of the application or interview process may contact, in confidence, recruitment@esb.ie. About ESB Join us in our mission to achieve a net\-zero electricity system by 2040\. ESB is Ireland’s leading energy utility, with activities spanning electricity generation, transmission and distribution, energy supply, energy services and international consultancy. Operating in Ireland, Northern Ireland and Great Britain, we invest over €1 billion each year to deliver a new energy future based on reliable, affordable zero\-carbon electricity.

description: **Position Description** Reporting to the Reporting Team Lead, the Reporting Analyst plays a key role within a commercially focused Pricing and Trading team. The role involves delivering accurate, timely tariff books and reporting to multiple stakeholders, while supporting wider Pricing and Trading activities. The team manages Electric Ireland’s reporting across market segments, including tariff book distribution for price changes (commercial or regulatory), customer volume reporting to Generation \& Trading, and provision of financial data for month\-end, forecasts, and budgeting. It also supports price reviews, monitoring processes, and regulatory reporting. This role suits an analytical, motivated individual seeking experience in a commercial environment. It requires strong collaboration with Pricing, Finance, Regulation, Business \& Residential Markets. This is a 12\-month fixed\-term maternity leave cover role. **Key Responsibilities** * Deliver accurate tariff books to support pricing and billing * Provide customer volume data for Electric Ireland and ESB Generation \& Trading * Produce timely month\-end, quarter\-end, and year\-end financial reports * Complete all regulatory reporting requirements * Conduct billing analysis and market monitoring to inform trading decisions * Assist with Green Source Verification, Fuel Mix Disclosure, and audits. Support annual emissions reporting (ETS2\) * Contribute to Renewable Heat Obligation (RHO) reporting **Essential** **Experience and Qualifications** * 2 \+ years’ experience of the energy industry, and knowledge of wholesale or retail electricity markets * Third level degree in a related discipline, or equivalent work experience. * Proven record of working as part of a team, providing support and guidance to other team members * Strong analytical and numerical skills * Ability to take initiative where appropriate, with ability to work to tight deadlines whilst maintaining accuracy and attention to detail. * Strong Microsoft Office skills specifically in the use of complex Excel spreadsheets * Proven ability to collaborate and engage with multiple stakeholders * Excellent communication and presentation skills (both written and verbal). **Desirable** * Experience of working in pricing and / or trading environment. * Knowledge of TPS, DFS systems. * Competent in the use of computer\-based modelling packages with experience in VBA, SPSS and SQL Server. **Location** This position will be based in Swift Square, Santry. *Smart Working at ESB is designed to make ‘in\-person’ time together as purposeful as possible while also enabling the effective use of digital tools and hybrid arrangements to preserve our commitment to flexibility. The successful candidate may work flexibly within the jurisdiction where they have been employed, with attendance at the workplace for in\-person collaboration **at least two days per week** . All Smart Working arrangements are subject to regular review based on the operational requirements of the role, the team, and the business.* **Reporting To** Reporting Team Lead, Pricing and Trading **Why Work with Us?** * Opportunity to lead Ireland’s transition to net zero carbon future * Career development through mentoring and training * Corporate Social Responsibility Opportunities * Sports and Social Clubs * Networking opportunities * Credit Union * Generous Pension * Access to staff well\-being programmes * Generous parental leave entitlements * Strong values\-based and inclusive culture * Strong commitment to diversity, equity and inclusion * Great team environment working to our Core Values: Courageous, Caring, Driven and Trusted **Salary** €49,800 \- €58,600 per annum (depending on experience) **Closing Date** *30th June 2026* **Interview** Candidates who are shortlisted for interview will complete a Behavioural Interview based on each of the following Behaviours, which underpin how we work at ESB and are critical to delivering our strategy: * Accountable and Ethical * Collaborative * Growing and Adaptive * Proactive * Respectful and Supportive More information available on Our People Promise **Notes** Your application will be held in reserve for 6 months should you be suitable for the role. The successful candidate will be required to evidence their right to work in the Republic of Ireland prior to commencement of employment.

title: Retail IT Support Analyst
description: **Job Description** The Retail IT Support Analyst keeps ARI’s retail technology running smoothly by delivering fast, effective support across systems, devices and end users. The role also drives continuous improvement by supporting projects, managing equipment and documenting business process changes, while deputising for the IT Manager when needed. **Responsibilities** * Provide first\-line support for ARI retail systems and end users, resolving incidents quickly and restoring service to minimise disruption to store operations. * Troubleshoot, diagnose, and coordinate resolution of POS, eCommerce, HHT and related hardware/software issues, ensuring stable and secure retail technology performance. * Log, prioritise, track and communicate progress on tickets, escalating effectively to internal specialist teams or external support partners to meet agreed service levels. * Identify recurring issues and trends, and implement or recommend preventative fixes to reduce repeat incidents and improve the end\-user experience. * Support and maintain retail IT equipment lifecycle activities (setup, configuration, replacements and basic inventory coordination) to keep stores operational and compliant with standards. * Partner with retail users to document business process and system change requirements, translating operational needs into clear technical inputs for delivery through the change request process. * Provide user guidance and informal training on systems, applications and best practices, enabling colleagues to use tools effectively and reducing avoidable support requests. * Assist the IT Manager with ongoing retail projects and deputise as required, ensuring continuity of day\-to\-day support and progress on agreed deliverables (no direct people management or budget ownership). **Qualifications** **Qualifications** Bachelor's degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, or a related field, or equivalent work experience. **Experience** 2\+ years of IT support experience, preferably in a retail or customer\-facing environment. Experience supporting POS systems, retail hardware, and end\-user systems. Project Management Experience assisting with or managing small IT projects, particularly in a retail setting – desirable but not essential **About Us** We're an airport industry leader with operations in four continents and over 3000 employees working together to ensure we deliver the best services for all our customers globally. We are a fast paced, energetic and diverse company. Offering careers that range from IT, Finance, Technical, Airport Operations to frontline security and customer focused roles and many more. Whether you are starting your career or looking for your next move, we have lots of great opportunities to choose from. **About The Team** ARI is one of the world’s longest established and experienced global travel retail companies. With expertise in developing business in new and existing markets, ARI owns and manages retail outlets in 27 airports in 14 countries throughout Europe, The Middle East, Asia Pacific, and North America. From opening the world’s first duty free in Shannon Airport in 1947, ARI now employs over 3,500 people worldwide and manages a turnover in excess of €1 billion. ARI is part of the Irish government\-owned daa group.

title: SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Specialist
description: We are seeking an experienced SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Consultant to join my client’s high\-performing IT Service Delivery team. This role will be responsible for the configuration, implementation, support, and continuous improvement of SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central, including Time Off, Role\-Based Permissions (RBP), IAS/IPS, reporting, and integrations. You will work closely with business stakeholders, HR teams, and technical teams to deliver scalable and efficient SuccessFactors solutions, supporting both ongoing operations and strategic transformation initiatives. **Key Responsibilities** * Lead workshops and stakeholder discussions to gather business requirements and define solution designs. * Configure and support SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central, Time Off, RBP, IAS/IPS, and reporting solutions. * Design, implement, and maintain security procedures and system access management across SAP SuccessFactors modules. * Manage Employee Central configuration, including Foundation Objects, MDF Objects, Data Models, Business Rules, Workflows, and Alerts. * Configure Position Management, Position\-to\-Job Synchronisation, Position Org Charts, and automated code generation. * Develop and maintain custom hire templates, employee data structures, and employee lifecycle processes. * Lead end\-to\-end testing activities, including coordination with third\-party vendors and business stakeholders. * Support data migration, cutover planning, deployment activities, and post\-go\-live support. * Configure and maintain role\-based permissions, country\-specific security models, and compliance requirements. * Provide expert guidance on Employee Central best practices, process improvements, and solution optimisation. **Essential Skills \& Experience** * SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Certification. * Minimum of 2–3 full lifecycle Employee Central implementations as a lead or core consultant. * Strong experience with Employee Central Time Off and absence management configuration. * Expertise in Foundation Objects, MDF Objects, HRIS Elements, HRIS Sync Mapping, and Provisioning. * Experience configuring Workflows, Business Rules, Alerts, Custom Notifications, and Document Generation. * Strong knowledge of Event Reason Derivation and Employee Data Management. * Experience with Global Assignments and Concurrent Employment. * Hands\-on experience with data migration, data validation, and employee master data management. * Experience with cutover planning, deployment activities, and post\-implementation support. * Excellent stakeholder management, communication, and workshop facilitation skills. **Desirable Skills \& Experience** * Experience developing test strategies, UAT plans, SIT plans, project plans, BPML documentation, and WBS structures. * Knowledge of system integration testing, test script creation, execution, and defect management. * Experience with Theme Manager, homepage customisation, and People Profile configuration. * Knowledge of Configuration Transport Centre, instance refreshes, and release management processes. * Experience configuring IAS/IPS, Azure Active Directory integration, IAM, and Single Sign\-On (SSO). * Strong reporting expertise, including Story Reports, Ad Hoc Reports, and Canvas Reports. * Experience integrating SuccessFactors with SAP S/4HANA. * Knowledge of Job Profile Builder, Skills, and Competencies frameworks. * Understanding of SuccessFactors Talent Management modules, including Recruiting, Onboarding, Learning, Performance \& Goals, and Compensation. * Experience with Integration Center, SAP CPI integrations, and third\-party vendor integrations. * Knowledge of LMS–Employee Central integrations. * Experience working with Jira and Agile delivery methodologies. **What We're Looking For** The ideal candidate will combine strong technical expertise with excellent stakeholder engagement skills. You will be comfortable leading workshops, translating business requirements into effective solutions, and delivering high\-quality outcomes in complex SAP SuccessFactors environments. If you are passionate about SAP SuccessFactors and enjoy working on impactful HR transformation initiatives, we'd love to hear from you. Salary dependent on candidate experience. Benefits: Annual Bonus Scheme. Contributory Pension. Private Medical Insurance. Life Assurance \& Long\-Term Disability. Employee Assistance Programme. 22 days annual leave \+ 10 public holidays. Relocation package. Continuous Learning \& Development. Access to extensive training \& certification resources. Lunch \& Learn sessions. Additional perks including company discounts, on\-site parking, and bike\-to\-work scheme **Based in Dublin. Hybrid (1\-2 days onsite per Month)** . Candidates must be eligible to work in Ireland/EU. For more information, please contact **David Coyle** at **01 635 1748** or email **david@methodius.com**

title: Sales & Student Advisor (Cantonese Speaker – Hong Kong Market)
description: About Us Established in 2003, Erin School of English empowers students to express themselves confidently in English, just as they do in their native language. We are committed to delivering a first\-class English language learning experience and preparing students to thrive in today's globalised world. Job Description We are looking for a motivated, customer\-focused, and results\-driven Sales \& Student Advisor to support the growth of our Hong Kong market. In this role, you will guide prospective students throughout their study abroad journey, helping them choose the right English programme and supporting them from the initial enquiry through to enrolment. You will play a key role in driving sales while delivering an outstanding student experience. Key Responsibilities * Build and maintain relationships with prospective and existing students from the Hong Kong market. * Respond to enquiries via phone calls, email, messaging platforms, and virtual meetings. * Prepare quotations and recommend suitable English programmes based on students' needs and goals. * Provide guidance on courses, accommodation options, life in Ireland, and student visa requirements. * Prospect, qualify, and follow up with leads to maximise enrolments and achieve sales targets. * Conduct market research to identify opportunities and trends within the Hong Kong market. * Support promotional initiatives and engage with prospective students through social media channels. * Deliver excellent customer service and ensure high levels of student satisfaction. * Handle enquiries and complaints professionally, empathetically, and efficiently. * Collaborate closely with the Administration, Marketing, Academic, and Sales teams to provide the best possible student experience. * Maintain accurate records and complete administrative tasks using internal systems and CRM tools. Requirements * Fluency in **Cantonese and English** (spoken and written) is essential. * Mandarin proficiency is considered an advantage. * Previous experience in Sales, Customer Service, Student Recruitment, or a similar client\-facing role. * Excellent communication, negotiation, and relationship\-building skills. * Strong customer\-service orientation with empathy and professionalism. * Proactive, self\-motivated, and results\-driven mindset. * Ability to work independently and manage multiple priorities in a fast\-paced environment. * Strong organisational skills and attention to detail. * Good knowledge of Microsoft Office, particularly Excel, and general computer literacy. Preferred Qualifications * Experience in international education, exchange programmes, or study abroad consultancy. * Familiarity with Irish student visa processes. * Experience using CRM systems. What We Offer * Opportunity to work in a dynamic and multicultural environment. * Career growth and development opportunities. * Competitive salary and commission structure. * The chance to help students achieve their dream of studying in Ireland. **Stamp 2 visa holders and entry\-level candidates with strong sales potential are encouraged to apply.** Job Type: Part\-time Pay: €10,000\.00\-€27,000\.00 per year Work Location: In person

title: Sales Advisor
description: Here at The Perfume Shop, people are at the heart of everything we do. We are always on the lookout for individuals with Talent, a Passion for what we do and a Spark to help achieve our vision. **The Role** Are you passionate about perfume, and love people? Do you want to be part of a supportive team, and thrive in a fast\-paced environment? Everyday, our expert Sales Advisors work hard to make sure our customers feel valued, confident and special. As a Sales Advisor, it is your role to create an outstanding customer experience and grow sales. Assisting with the daily operational running of the store, you are customer focused, working as part of an amazing and supportive team to deliver against key targets that drive the success of your store. Contracted Hours: 12 Contract Type: Permanent Contract Pay Rate: €10\.02 \- €15\.77 **What You Will Do** You will greet customers and give great service by listening to their needs We’re proud to be the perfume experts, so you’ll be providing advice to customers on their perfume choices. Don’t worry, we’ll make sure you get all the training you need You will help the team to achieve and exceed store and team targets – and there are fantastic incentives and prizes on offer to recognise success Every customer is a VIP at The Perfume Shop, so you’ll offer every customer a free reward card to maximise their future offers and potential savings **What You Will Bring** We believe that you bring Talent, Passion and Spark and in return we will give you the skills and training in perfume to succeed! **It really helps if you;** Have a talent for getting along with people Have a passion for teamwork, and being successful together Have the spark to love learning about perfume Can be flexible with your work patterns **What You Will Get** * We pay commission at Christmas based on store sales and the number of hours worked * We’ll ensure you work regular hours and get your rota at least 4 weeks in advance so you can plan your life outside work * 32 days holiday (pro rata depending on contract hours) * You’re also a VIP to us, so you’ll get 20% off perfume and be able to take part in any customer offers on top * Additional discounts with our AS Watson partners including Superdrug and 3 Mobile * The opportunity to win some great prizes and incentives based on personal, team and store performance * Continuous learning and training to ensure you are always developing * Access to Wagestream, to support your financial wellbeing needs, giving you more control and flexibility with your pay. * Pension, health and wellbeing benefits, \+ more! **We are passionate about ‘Growing Our Own’, so we always encourage our employees to progress internally.** *We thank you for your interest, however due to the high volumes of applications, only those successful will be contacted. We do still encourage you to continue applying to any roles of interest you see advertised.* The Perfume Shop is an equal opportunity employer that is committed to supporting diversity and inclusion within our work environment. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, colour, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status. We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. The Perfume Shop is part of AS Watson Group, the world’s largest international health and beauty retailer operating over 16,500 stores under 12 retail brands in 28 markets, with over 130,000 employees worldwide. For the fiscal year 2023, AS Watson Group recorded revenue of US$23 billion. Every year, it is serving over 5\.5 billion shoppers via its O\+O (Offline plus Online) platforms, providing tech\-enabled retail experience to customers offline and online.

title: Sales Advisor
description: At DID Electrical, we are a Guaranteed Irish and proud of our reputation for providing superior customer service and guaranteed lowest prices. Our first family\-owned store opened in Mountjoy square in 1968, since then we have expanded our family with 23 Irish owned stores nationwide and our online store! With an amazing history and an exciting future, everything we do is embedded in strong family values. We are looking for talented and driven Part Time Sales to join our Mullingar Branch. Does this sound like you? If so, we want to hear from you! **Does this sound ''Electric'' to you?** Competitive commission earning opportunities (uncapped). One of the market leaders in commission rates. Track record of progressing and promoting talent from within through ongoing training and development opportunities. Fun and collaborative work environments Opportunity to gain comprehensive product knowledge across a range of leading brands in electrical retail Fantastic Staff and Friends \& Family Discounts Employee recognition rewards We are entrepreneurial and growing We are recruiting for dynamic and enthusiastic individuals into the role of full\-time Sales Advisor in our Mullingar Branch. Our Sales Advisors are ambitious, provide outstanding customer service, and thrive in the electrical retail industry. **A "Fully Charged" Sales Advisor will:** Provide a superior customer experience by matching the customer's needs through product demonstration and the ability to close the sale. Achieve sales targets and maximise commission earning potential. Work as a team to achieve branch targets through KPIs. Be a brand ambassador. With ongoing training opportunities, have first class product knowledge \& innovation in new and exciting tech brands and leading kitchen appliance market leaders. Merchandise a range of products and create exciting store displays for our customers Update pricing and maintain store standards. To influence and enhance the customer journey and to go the extra mile to build trust and loyalty with our customers. Perform other duties as required. **Are you ''Switched on''?** Dynamic, Friendly, energetic and strong positive work ethic. Ambitious and results driven with an appetite for sales. Ability to work in a fast\-paced collaborative sales environment. Eagar to expand product knowledge with a range of exciting brands. Adaptable to new technology trends and exciting new product ranges. Ability to understand the customer's needs, utilising strong communication skills. PC Literate. Proficient knowledge of MS Office, Word, Outlook and Excel is desirable 1\-3 years' experience in a previous sales or customer\-facing role would be an advantage but not essential. We need employees to be flexible about when they work, covering store opening hours, including days, evenings, weekends and public holidays

title: Sales Associate
description: Ref \# W179066 State/Region Kildare Department Retail Stores Location Ireland City Kildare **COMPANY DESCRIPTION** ----------------------- Ralph Lauren Corporation (NYSE:RL) is a global leader in the design, marketing and distribution of premium lifestyle products in five categories: apparel, accessories, home, fragrances, and hospitality. For more than 50 years, Ralph Lauren's reputation and distinctive image have been consistently developed across an expanding number of products, brands and international markets. The Company's brand names, which include Ralph Lauren, Ralph Lauren Collection, Ralph Lauren Purple Label, Polo Ralph Lauren, Double RL, Lauren Ralph Lauren, Polo Ralph Lauren Children, Chaps, among others, constitute one of the world's most widely recognized families of consumer brands. At Ralph Lauren, we unite and inspire the communities within our company as well as those in which we serve by amplifying voices and perspectives to create a culture of belonging, ensuring inclusion, and fairness for all. We foster a culture of inclusion through: Talent, Education \& Communication, Employee Groups and Celebration. **POSITION OVERVIEW** --------------------- **WHAT WE OFFER** * The opportunity to work for an iconic and unique lifestyle brand * Exciting career opportunities * Competitive benefits package and discounts * An environment where you can bring your whole self to work **ESSENTIAL DUTIES \& RESPONSIBILITIES** ---------------------------------------- **WHAT YOU'LL DO** **One team focused on the consumer** * Deliver an extraordinary customer experience to drive sales * Communicate consistently with the team to ensure an excellent customer service * Provide product and customer feedback to managers **Ralph Lauren Brand Ambassador** * Provide a welcoming and elevated experience * Embody the Ralph Lauren values and spirit * Demonstrate a customer\-first culture **Operations** * Use the Ralph Lauren store systems and procedures to enhance selling efficiencies and to build the customer data\-base * Maintain established operational and merchandising store standards * Store maintenance **EXPERIENCE, SKILLS \& KNOWLEDGE** ----------------------------------- **WHAT YOU ARE** * Energetic, passionate, and results\-driven team player * Experience in a fast\-paced environment * Excellent communication skills

title: Sales Associate
description: **Our Sales Associates help our customers Celebrate Life \& Express Love!** --------------------------------------------------------------------------- Our store teams are made up of customer\-first people with a real passion for outstanding service, creating amazing experiences and lasting memories for our customers. Our team members work hard to develop outstanding product knowledge and real jewellery expertise that they can share with our customers. As a committed and dedicated member of the team, you’ll play a key role in helping the store to maintain performance and meet sales targets. As new products are introduced in store, you will build your knowledge to ensure that you can deal with every type of customer enquiry. **Your background** You’ll have a passion for putting the customer first and creating memorable customer experiences. While you may not specifically have jewellery experience, you will certainly have an interest in jewellery products and brands. A positive, ‘can\-do’ attitude is essential, with a natural ability for striking up a conversation with a diverse range of customers. **What's next?** When you submit your application, you will be invited to complete a short online assessment. If successful, you will be asked to complete a short video which gives you the opportunity to tell us a little more about you and why you would like to join us. Successful applicants will be contacted by the store manager inviting you to the final stage interviews. We recommend to regularly check your emails to ensure you don’t miss any updates with your application. **Your rewards** ---------------- We offer a competitive salary and benefits, including sales incentives and a staff discount. The nature of our business also means you can sometimes work additional hours at times like peak trading periods, Bank Holidays and weekends. What is also really amazing about Signet are the career development opportunities. If you’ve got potential, we’ll help you fulfil it. We’ve got the training and development programmes in place to really help you make the most of your talent! **Be part of something special!** --------------------------------- Signet is the name behind H. Samuel and Ernest Jones. Together, we have more than 270 stores in the UK and Republic of Ireland. But that’s not all, we are part of the world’s largest retailer of diamond jewellery with approximately 2,800 stores. Just imagine where your career could take you **Signet Jewelers (H. Samuel \& Ernest Jones) is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion.** We welcome applications from all sections of the community and are proud to have achieved Disability Confident Committed status (Level 1\). All employment is decided on the basis of qualifications, merit and business need.

title: Sales Care Co-Ordinator
description: **Job****Description** Join Us on a Mission to Transform Lives Beyondbmi is looking for a passionate and driven Sales Executive (Care Co\-ordinator) to join our growing team. This is an exciting opportunity to be part of a purpose\-driven, fast\-growing healthtech company that’s redefining how obesity is treated across Europe. **About Beyondbmi** Beyondbmi is a fast\-growing healthtech company delivering evidence\-based, medical weight management services. We combine clinical expertise, behavioural science, and technology to support people on their weight loss journey. As a regulated healthcare provider, we are committed to operational excellence, compliance, and a seamless user experience. **Who We’re Looking For** You’re a people\-first sales professional with a genuine interest in healthcare and wellbeing. You’re excited to contribute to a high\-impact mission, love talking to clients, and thrive in a fast\-paced, target\-driven environment. This role does not involve cold calling. All leads come from individuals who have expressed interest in our services. **Key Responsibilities** Manage Inbound Leads: Follow up on inquiries from prospective clients who have expressed an interest in the Beyondbmi offering, asking questions to understand the prospective clients needs and challenges. Respond to inquiries via email and phone, and follow up on completed online forms. Conduct Consultations: Engage in compassionate, informed conversations to assess client needs and determine programme suitability. Own Your Pipeline: Manage and follow up with all leads to guide them through the sign\-up process. Achieve Results: Meet and exceed weekly sales targets in a supportive, mission\-driven environment. Educate \& Empower: Clearly explain Beyond BMI’s unique, medically\-managed weight loss solution, and help remove stigma, shame, and bias around obesity. Stay Informed: Keep up\-to\-date on developments in obesity care, behavioural change, and health innovation. Collaborate \& Grow: Be an active member of a dynamic team, helping shape and scale a business with ambitious goals. **About You** Experienced: 2 years of sales experience, ideally in healthcare, wellness, lifestyle, or a mission\-driven environment. Compassionate Communicator: Excellent verbal and written communication skills with a warm, empathetic approach. Sales Confident: Comfortable with targets and asking for the sale—without pressure or shame. Healthcare Curious: Passionate about health equity and excited to learn about obesity as a disease. Organised \& Tech\-Savvy: Strong admin skills and proficient in tools like MS Word, Teams, Excel, and PowerPoint. CRM / Hubspot experience is a major plus. Team\-Oriented: A collaborative mindset with a positive attitude, ready to thrive in a remote, high\-performing team. Work\-Eligible: You must have the right to work in Ireland. **Bonus Points** Experience or background in counselling, nutrition, fitness, or weight management. Previous exposure to healthtech, telehealth, or startup environments. Why Beyondbmi? We’re a small but mighty team working to remove the shame, stigma, and barriers people face in accessing care for obesity. As we scale our impact, we’re looking for people who are excited to grow with us. If this sounds like you, we’d love to hear from you.

title: Sales Development Representative - German
description: **Secure Every Identity, from AI to Human** Identity is the key to unlocking the potential of AI. Okta secures AI by building the trusted, neutral infrastructure that enables organizations to safely embrace this new era. This work requires a relentless drive to solve complex challenges with real\-world stakes. We are looking for builders and owners who operate with speed and urgency and execute with excellence. This is an opportunity to do career\-defining work. We're all in on this mission. If you are too, let's talk. peripheral issues. * Run conference meetings and Townhalls * Manage user lifecycle tasks across Entra ID, Microsoft 365, and third\-party business platforms. * Provision and manage devices via Intune/Jamf. * Log, update and resolve tickets within SLA targets. * Maintain accurate documentation and runbooks. * Contribute to knowledge base expansion and known\-error documentation. * Support IT rollouts, pilots and device refresh programmes. * Support security initiatives and low\-risk change processes. * Identify recurring issues, analyse support trends, and recommend proactive improvements to enhance service quality and reduce operational friction * Collaborate with Infrastructure and Security teams on operational and project\-based activities * Support and adopt AI\-assisted workplace title: Site Reliability Engineer (SRE/ DevOps) - Engineering Productivity
description: **Company Description** Arista Networks is an industry leader in data\-driven, client\-to\-cloud networking for large data center, campus and routing environments. What sets us apart is our relentless pursuit of innovation. We leverage the latest advancements in cloud computing, artificial intelligence, and software\-defined networking to provide our clients with a competitive edge in an increasingly interconnected world. Our solutions are designed to not only meet the current demands of the digital landscape but to also anticipate and adapt to future challenges. At Arista we value the diversity of thought and perspectives that each employee brings to the table. We believe that fostering an inclusive environment, where individuals from various backgrounds and experiences feel welcome, is essential for driving creativity and innovation. Our commitment to excellence has earned us several prestigious awards, such as Best Engineering Team, Best Company for Diversity, Compensation, and Work\-Life Balance. At Arista, we take pride in our track record of success and strive to maintain the highest standards of quality and performance in everything we do. **Job Description** **Who You'll Work With** Arista Networks is looking for a skilled professional for our Engineering Productivity (EngProd) team to help maintain and support our rapidly expanding infrastructure and internal user base. The ideal candidate is someone who can wear many hats, is versatile and is enthusiastic about learning new technologies. As a part of the software engineering team, you will work with other team members to design, build and administer secure, scalable and fault\-tolerant tools and infrastructure in a hybrid cloud environment. Working in the EngProd group, you will collaborate and work with other engineers to design, build, scale, and operate the systems used by Arista's product development teams. These systems are based on industry\-standards, including Ansible, Artifactory, Gerrit, Jenkins, Kubernetes, Grafana, Spinnaker, MySQL, ElasticSearch, Google Cloud, Varnish, Perforce, Gerrit etc, 3rd party storage appliances, as well as internal systems developed from the ground\-up to automate CI/CD, testing, analysis, and visualization. **What You'll Do** * Build, deploy safely and incrementally, and operate critical production systems with focus on scalability, reliability, observability, performance and security. * Build automation to remove toil and proactively monitor, respond to, and enhance alerts with automated handling. * Create and maintain incident response runbooks, triage platform and infrastructural issues, and write postmortem documents to prevent recurring incidents. * Plan and communicate maintenance windows on production systems while engaging with 3rd party vendor support as needed. * Work with Arista's product development teams to identify infrastructural bottlenecks and design solutions to enhance developer experience and workflow efficiency. * Survey and adopt best practices around infrastructure and platform design to maintain secure, scalable and fault\-tolerant systems, including studying OSS system implementations for better triage and resolution. \#UNIX \#Go \#Python \#Shell Scripting \#Ansible \#Docker \#Kubernetes **Qualifications** Essential Skills * At least BSc Computer Science or Engineering \+ 3 years’ experience, MS Computer Science or Engineering \+ 3 years’ experience, or equivalent work experience. * Knowledge of one or more of Go, Python, shell scripting to be able to implement medium complexity automation workflows. * Knowledge of Linux (or UNIX) from administration and debugging perspective * Hands\-on experience in operating software systems (infrastructure, complex applications etc) at scale * Experience in server provisioning (esp from storage and networking perspective). * Strong problem solving and software troubleshooting skills * Experience with infrastructure\-as\-code **Desired Skills** * Experience managing databases \- mariadb, postgres, mongodb etc * Experience with docker and virtualization technologies \- kvm, qemu, kata\-containers etc * Experience managing monitoring stack \- Prometheus, Loki, Tempo, InfluxDB, Grafana, Thanos etc * Experience managing ElasticSearch clusters * Experience managing Artifactory, docker registry etc * Experience managing CI/CD systems like ArgoCD, Spinnaker etc * Experience managing version control systems like Perforce, Gerrit etc * Experience with infrastructure\-as\-code frameworks like Ansible * Experience managing large Java applications * Experience in storage infrastructure management eg: NAS, SAN, Ceph etc

title: Social Care Worker/ Autism Practitioner
description: **Social Care Worker/ Autism Practitioner** =========================================== **North Dublin** ---------------- ### **Specified Purpose Contract** **Join Our Team at Gheel Autism Services** **Social Care Worker/Autism Practitioner – Day Support Team, North Dublin,** * **Location:** Fairview Day Support * **Contract:** Specified Purpose Contract full\-time (39 hours/week) * **Salary Range:** €37,096 – €53,674 per annum * **Salary Growth:** Sector\-wide pay agreement will provide for a 2% increase from the 1st of April 2026\. * **Driving Licence:** Full, clean licence preferred but not essential **Who We Are** At Gheel Autism Services, we are dedicated to supporting individuals with autism through their life journey. Our services include day and vocational opportunities, outreach support, and various supported living options. We take a strengths\-based approach, tailoring personalised plans to meet each individual’s unique needs. Our team is committed to fostering independence, social inclusion, and overall well\-being, ensuring that each person achieves outcomes that are meaningful to them. As part of the Gheel team, you will receive: * Professional Growth: Training, career progression, and education support. * Wellbeing Support: Employee Assistance Programme, reflective practice, and supportive supervision. * Annual Leave: 25 days, plus various leave types such as paid maternity and paternity leave (dependant on service). * Financial Benefits: Competitive salary, annual increments, pension * Travel \& Discounts: Bike\-to\-Work and an Employee Discount Scheme. **About the Role** Gheel Autism Services provides person\-cantered day opportunities across North and South Dublin, offering unique and tailored supports based on the choices, needs, and aspirations of the individuals we support. We are currently hiring for one specified purpose contract Social Care Worker/Autism Practitioner positions as part of our Day Supports Team in Fairview, Dublin 3\.
